name: Feynman / Analogy
description: Simple everyday analogy, assume no prior knowledge.
promptTemplate:
Explain this to me using a simple, everyday analogy, as if I know nothing about the topic yet. Question: {{question}}

Context: {{context}}

name: Socratic Follow-up
description: Ask guiding questions before answering, to force active reasoning.
promptTemplate:
Before answering, ask me 2-3 guiding questions that would help me reason my way to the answer myself. Then reveal the full answer. Question: {{question}}

Context: {{context}}

name: First-Principles Breakdown
description: Build up from underlying mechanisms step by step.
promptTemplate:
Break this down from first principles / underlying mechanisms, step by step, building up to the full answer. Question: {{question}}

Context: {{context}}

name: Exam-Style Application
description: Frame the answer around how it actually gets tested.
promptTemplate:
Answer this the way I'd need to apply it on an MCAT passage question. Focus on how this concept gets tested, common traps, and include one worked example. Question: {{question}}

Context: {{context}}

name: Mnemonic / Memory Hook
description: Concise answer plus a memory device.
promptTemplate:
Answer this concisely, then give me a mnemonic, memory hook, or vivid image I can use to remember it. Question: {{question}}

Context: {{context}}
